zh.title: 以太坊/美元技术分析：高位震荡偏多整理
zh.content:
当前ETH价格维持在1723附近，短线呈现高位震荡格局，整体趋势偏多但动能放缓，多空进入均衡博弈阶段运行中等待方向选择信号

MACD指标为正值4.93显示多头仍占优势，但信号线缺失使动量确认不足。价格站稳20日均线1675上方，趋势结构保持上行延续偏强

上方阻力集中在1728一线，若突破将打开进一步上行空间，下方1713形成短期支撑，跌破则回测1675均线支撑区域关注有效突破信号强弱变化

短线建议以回调低吸为主，避免追高操作，若价格回踩1675附近可分批布局多单，跌破支撑则及时止损观望等待进一步信号确认为宜

en.title: Ethereum vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
Ethereum is showing a mild upward consolidation after holding gains around 1723. Price action remains relatively tight within the 1713–1728 range, suggesting short-term equilibrium between buyers and sellers. The fact that ETH is trading above its 20-day simple moving average near 1675 indicates a broader bullish structure is still intact. Momentum is stabilizing rather than accelerating, pointing to a potential buildup phase before the next directional move.

MACD at 4.93 indicates underlying bullish momentum, though the lack of a visible signal line reading limits full crossover confirmation. Still, price stability above recent intraday supports suggests buyers are consistently defending dips. Without RSI data, momentum inference relies on structure, which currently shows higher lows and tightening consolidation. This typically reflects compression before volatility expansion, keeping the near-term bias cautiously positive.

Immediate support is established at 1713, the recent session low, followed by stronger structural backing at the 20-day SMA near 1675. A deeper correction could expose the 1650 area if selling pressure increases. On the upside, resistance is clearly defined at 1728, with a breakout potentially unlocking momentum toward 1750 and then 1780. Price is effectively coiling between defined technical boundaries.

From a trading perspective, conditions favor range-based strategies until a decisive breakout occurs. Long positions near support with tight risk controls below 1710 offer favorable asymmetry, especially if bullish structure holds. Alternatively, a confirmed break above 1730 would signal continuation strength and justify momentum entries. Until then, disciplined positioning around range extremes remains the most efficient approach.
